methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $123M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Neura Robotics is a Series B robotics & manufacturing company founded by David Reger that has raised $123.3M to date and carries a current valuation of €2.6B. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private companies and 1 public company in the robotics sector at the same stage. The peer set spans 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), and Fairmat ($350M), with a median valuation of $1.1B and a range from $300M to $10.0B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, anchored on $123M total raised as a proxy, applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $234M. The peer-median approach across six same-sector, same-stage companies produces $1.1B. These methods produce a low-to-high range of $199M to $1.2B, with a median of $655M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $199M to $1.2B, with a central estimate around $655M. Material upside exists toward the peer median of $1.1B if the company demonstrates execution comparable to leading-stage peers. Key uncertainties include the timing and scale of commercialization milestones and the wide valuation dispersion within the peer set, which spans nearly a 34x range.
